input.prompt:
I need to create a C# MCP server that uses HTTP transport for deployment
as a web service. It should expose both tools and resources (for example,
a resource that returns configuration data). Show me how to set up the
HTTP transport with MapMcp and implement a resource.

as a web service. It should expose both tools and resources (for example,
a resource that returns configuration data). Show me how to set up the
HTTP transport with MapMcp and implement a resource.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to create a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using HTTP transport with `MapMcp` and implement both tools and resources. Let me create a working example project for them.

arguments.name: explore-mcp-apis
arguments.prompt:
I need to understand the APIs available in the ModelContextProtocol and ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore NuGet packages (version 1.2.0) for building an HTTP-based MCP server in C#.

Please examine these XML doc files and DLLs to answer:

1. What extension methods exist in ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore? Specifically look for `MapMcp` and any HTTP transport setup methods. Check `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.xml`

2. How do you register MCP server services (AddMcpServer)? What options/configuration does it support? Check `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml`

3. How do you define MCP resources? Look for attributes like `McpServerResource`, `McpServerResourceTemplate`, or interfaces for resource handling. Search the XML docs for anything related to "resource".

4. How do you define MCP tools? Look for `McpServerTool` attribute or similar.

5. What is the correct pattern for `AddMcpServer` — does it take a configure callback? What are the options available (ServerInfo, Capabilities, etc.)?

Search both XML files thoroughly. Also run: `strings /home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.dll | grep -i 'McpServer\|Resource\|MapMcp' | sort -u | head -60` and the same for the AspNetCore DLL to find type names.

691.             <summary>

696.             <summary>
697.             Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport.
698.             </summary>
699.             <param name="endpoints">The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints.</param>
700.             <param name="pattern">The route pattern prefix to map to.</param>
701.             <returns>Returns a builder for configuring additional endpoint conventions like authorization policies.</returns>
702.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The required MCP services have not been registered. Ensure <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions.WithHttpTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions})"/> has been called during application startup.</exception>
703.             <remarks>
704.             For details about the Streamable HTTP transport, see the <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports#streamable-http">2025-11-25 protocol specification</see>.
705.             When legacy SSE is enabled via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.HttpServerTransportOptions.EnableLegacySse"/>, this method also maps legacy SSE endpoints at the path "/sse" and "/message". For details about the HTTP with SSE transport, see the <see href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">2024-11-05 protocol specification</see>.
706.             </remarks>
707.         </member>

result.content: Validates that is set. Represents configuration options for , which implements the Streamable HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol. See the protocol specification for details on the Streamable HTTP transport. For details on the Streamable HTTP transport, see the protocol specification. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session with access to the of the request that initiated the session. In stateful mode (the default), this callback is invoked once per session when the client sends the initialize request. In mode, it is invoked on every HTTP request because each request creates a fresh server context. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually. This callback is useful for running logic before a session starts and after it completes. The parameter comes from the request that initiated the session (e.g., the initialize request) and may not be usable after starts, since that request will have already completed. Consider using instead, which provides access to the of the initializing request with fewer known issues. This API is experimental and may be removed or change signatures in a future release. -- Gets or sets the time provider that's used for testing the . Post-configures by resolving services from DI when they haven't been explicitly set on the options. Post-configures by resolving services from DI when they haven't been explicitly set on the options. Provides hooks for persisting and restoring MCP session initialization data, enabling session migration across server instances. When an MCP server is horizontally scaled, stateful sessions are bound to a single process. If that process restarts or scales down, the session is lost. By implementing this interface and registering it with DI, you can persist the initialization handshake data and restore it when a client reconnects to a different server instance with its existing Mcp-Session-Id. This does not solve the session-affinity problem for in-flight server-to-client requests (such as sampling or elicitation). Responses to those requests must still be routed to the process that created the request. This interface only enables migration of idle sessions by persisting the data established during the initialization handshake. Called after a session has been successfully initialized via the MCP initialization handshake. Use this to persist the (which includes client capabilities, client info, and protocol version) to an external store so the session can be migrated to another server instance later via .

13.             <summary>
14.             Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks
15.             and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
16.             </summary>
17.             <param name="timestamp">The timestamp to embed in the identifier.</param>
18.             <returns>A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.</returns>
19.             <remarks>
20.             <para>
21.             This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values:
22.             - High 64 bits: <see cref="P:System.DateTimeOffset.Ticks"/> from the timestamp
23.             - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter
24.             </para>
25.             <para>
26.             The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically,
27.             which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike <c>Guid.CreateVersion7</c>,
28.             which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees
29.             strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.

result.content: 181. 182. 183. Represents configuration options for , 184. which implements the Streamable HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol. 185. See the protocol specification for details on the Streamable HTTP transport. 186. 187. 188. For details on the Streamable HTTP transport, see the protocol specification. 189. 190. 191. 192. 193. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session 194. with access to the of the request that initiated the session. 195. 196. 197. In stateful mode (the default), this callback is invoked once per session when the client sends the 198. initialize request. In mode, it is invoked on every HTTP request 199. because each request creates a fresh server context. 200. 201. 202. 203. 204. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually. 205. 206. 207. This callback is useful for running logic before a session starts and after it completes. 208. 209. The parameter comes from the request that initiated the session (e.g., the 210. initialize request) and may not be usable after starts, since that 211. request will have already completed. 212. 213. 214. Consider using instead, which provides access to the 215. of the initializing request with fewer known issues. 216. 217. 218. This API is experimental and may be removed or change signatures in a future release. 219. 220. 221. 222. 223. 224. Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the server runs in a stateless mode that doesn't track state between requests, 225. allowing for load balancing without session affinity. 226. 227. 228. if the server runs in a stateless mode; if the server tracks state between requests. The default is . 229. 230. 231. If , will be null, and the "MCP-Session-Id" header will not be used, 232. the will be called once for each request, and the "/sse" endpoint will be disabled. 233. Unsolicited server-to-client messages and all server-to-client requests are also unsupported, because any responses 234. might arrive at another ASP.NET Core application process. 235. Client sampling, elicitation, and roots capabilities are also disabled in stateless mode, because the server cannot make requests. 236. 237. 238. 239. 240. Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the server maps legacy SSE endpoints (/sse and /message) 241. for backward compatibility with clients that do not support the Streamable HTTP transport. 242. 243. 244. to map the legacy SSE endpoints; to disable them. The default is . 245. 246. 247. 248. The legacy SSE transport separates request and response channels: clients POST JSON-RPC messages 249. to /message and receive responses through a long-lived GET SSE stream on /sse. 250. Because the POST endpoint returns 202 Accepted immediately, there is no HTTP-level 251. backpressure on handler concurrency — unlike Streamable HTTP, where each POST is held open 252. until the handler responds. 253. 254. 255. Use Streamable HTTP instead whenever possible. If you must support legacy SSE clients, 256. enable this property only for completely trusted clients in isolated processes, and apply 257. HTTP rate-limiting middleware and reverse proxy limits to compensate for the lack of 258. built-in backpressure. 259. 260. 261. Setting this to while is also 262. throws an at startup, because SSE requires in-memory session state. 263. 264. 265. This property can also be enabled via the ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.EnableLegacySse 266. switch. 267. 268. 269. 270. 271. 272. Gets or sets the event store for resumability support. 273. When set, events are stored and can be replayed when clients reconnect with a Last-Event-ID header. 274. 275. 276. When configured, the server will: 277. 278. Generate unique event IDs for each SSE message 279. Store events for later replay 280. Replay missed events when a client reconnects with a Last-Event-ID header 281. Send priming events to establish resumability before any actual messages 282. 283. 284. This can be set directly, or an can be registered in DI. 285. If this property is not set, the server will attempt to resolve an from DI. 286. 287. 288. 289. 290. 291. If a request arrives with a session ID that is not found locally, the handler 297. is consulted to determine if the session can be migrated from another instance. 298. 299. 300. This can be set directly, or an can be registered in DI. 301. If this property is not set, the server will attempt to resolve an from DI. 302. 303. 304. 305. 306. 307. The default is . 311. 312. 313. If , handlers like tools get called with the 314. belonging to the corresponding HTTP request, which can change throughout the MCP session. 315. data.content:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide a comprehensive answer:

## Comprehensive API Reference: ModelContextProtocol v1.2.0 NuGet Packages

Based on my examination of the XML documentation in both NuGet packages, here's the complete reference for building HTTP-based MCP servers in C#:

---

### **1. Extension Methods in ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore**

#### **MapMcp** - HTTP Endpoint Registration
Located in `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.McpEndpointRouteBuilderExtensions`

```csharp
MapMcp(IEndpointRouteBuilder endpoints, string pattern)
```

**Purpose**: Sets up endpoints for handling MCP Streamable HTTP transport
- **Parameters**:
  - `endpoints`: The web application to attach MCP HTTP endpoints
  - `pattern`: Route pattern prefix to map to (e.g., "/mcp")
- **Returns**: An endpoint builder for configuring conventions like authorization policies
- **Protocol Spec**: 2025-11-25 Streamable HTTP transport
- **Legacy Support**: When `EnableLegacySse` is enabled, also maps `/sse` and `/message` endpoints (2024-11-05 HTTP with SSE)
- **Exception**: Throws `InvalidOperationException` if `WithHttpTransport()` hasn't been called during startup

#### **WithHttpTransport** - HTTP Transport Configuration
Located in `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.HttpMcpServerBuilderExtensions`

```csharp
WithHttpTransport(IMcpServerBuilder builder, Action<HttpServerTransportOptions> configureOptions)
```

**Purpose**: Adds services for handling MCP requests via Streamable HTTP transport
- **Parameters**:
  - `builder`: The MCP server builder instance
  - `configureOptions`: Callback to configure HTTP transport options
- **Returns**: The builder for chaining
- **Note**: Prerequisite for `MapMcp()` to function

#### **AddAuthorizationFilters** - Authorization Support
```csharp
AddAuthorizationFilters(IMcpServerBuilder builder)
```

**Purpose**: Adds authorization filters to support `[Authorize]` attributes on tools, prompts, and resources
- Should always be called when using ASP.NET Core integration
- Respects `[Authorize]` and `[AllowAnonymous]` attributes

---

### **2. Service Registration: AddMcpServer**

Located in `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions`

```csharp
AddMcpServer(IServiceCollection services, Action<McpServerOptions> configureOptions)
```

**Purpose**: Registers the MCP server to the DI container
- **Parameters**:
  - `services`: The service collection to add the server to
  - `configureOptions`: Optional callback to configure `McpServerOptions`
- **Returns**: `IMcpServerBuilder` for fluent configuration of server components

**Basic Pattern**:
```csharp
services
    .AddMcpServer(options => {
        // Configure server options here
    })
    .WithTools<MyTools>()
    .WithResources<MyResources>()
    .WithHttpTransport(opts => {
        // Configure HTTP transport
    });
```

---

### **3. McpServerOptions Configuration**

While specific property documentation wasn't fully visible in the XML search, based on the API signatures and types found, `McpServerOptions` supports:

- **ConfigureSessionOptions**: Async callback to configure per-session server options with `HttpContext` access
- **RunSessionHandler**: Async callback for running logic before/after MCP sessions (experimental)
- **Capabilities**: Server capabilities configuration (see `ServerCapabilities` type)

See also: `HttpServerTransportOptions.ConfigureSessionOptions` for session-level configuration

---

### **4. HttpServerTransportOptions Properties**

This is the primary configuration object for HTTP-based MCP servers:

| Property | Type | Default | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|---------|
| **ConfigureSessionOptions** | `Func<HttpContext, McpServerOptions, Task>` | null | Async callback to configure per-session server options. Invoked once per session (stateful) or every request (stateless) |
| **RunSessionHandler** | `Func<HttpContext, McpServer, Task>` | null | Optional callback for running new sessions with pre/post-session logic (experimental) |
| **Stateless** | `bool` | `false` | Enables stateless mode (no session tracking, load-balancing friendly) |
| **EnableLegacySse** | `bool` | `false` | Maps legacy SSE endpoints (`/sse`, `/message`) for backward compatibility |
| **EventStreamStore** | `ISseEventStreamStore` | null | Event store for SSE resumability support |
| **SessionMigrationHandler** | `ISessionMigrationHandler` | null | Handler for cross-instance session migration |
| **PerSessionExecutionContext** | `bool` | `false` | Use single execution context for entire session vs. per-request context |
| **IdleTimeout** | `TimeSpan` | 2 hours | Session timeout between active requests |
| **MaxIdleSessionCount** | `int` | 10,000 | Maximum idle sessions to track in memory |
| **TimeProvider** | `TimeProvider` | null | Time provider for testing idle timeouts |

**Stateless Mode Notes**:
- Session ID (`Mcp-Session-Id` header) is not used
- Each request creates a fresh server context
- Unsolicited server-to-client messages and requests are disabled
- Client sampling, elicitation, and roots capabilities are disabled

---

### **5. Defining MCP Resources**

#### **Attribute-Based Approach** (Recommended)

**Class-Level Attribute**: `McpServerResourceTypeAttribute`
```csharp
[McpServerResourceType]
public class MyResources
{
    [McpServerResource]
    public async Task<string> ReadMyResource()
    {
        return "resource content";
    }
}
```

**Member-Level Attribute**: `McpServerResourceAttribute`
- Applied to methods and properties that represent resources
- Supports both static and instance members
- Resource templates are discoverable via `list_resourceTemplates` request
- Resources can be invoked via `read_resource` request

#### **Registration Methods**

1. **Generic type registration** (Native AOT compatible):
   ```csharp
   builder.WithResources<MyResources>()
   ```

2. **Instance-based registration**:
   ```csharp
   builder.WithResources<MyResources>(resourceInstance)
   ```

3. **Collection registration**:
   ```csharp
   builder.WithResources(IEnumerable<McpServerResource> resources)
   ```

4. **Type collection registration**:
   ```csharp
   builder.WithResources(IEnumerable<Type> resourceTypes)
   ```

5. **Assembly-based (reflection)**:
   ```csharp
   builder.WithResourcesFromAssembly(assembly)
   ```

**Notes**:
- For instance methods, a new instance is created for each resource invocation
- Reflection-based methods may not work in Native AOT scenarios
- Use generic methods for Native AOT compatibility

---

### **6. Defining MCP Tools**

#### **Attribute-Based Approach**

**Class-Level Attribute**: `McpServerToolTypeAttribute`
```csharp
[McpServerToolType]
public class MyTools
{
    [McpServerTool]
    [Description("Gets the weather")]
    public async Task<string> GetWeather(string location)
    {
        return $"Weather for {location}";
    }
}
```

**Method-Level Attribute**: `McpServerToolAttribute`
- Applied to methods representing tools
- Supports both public and non-public methods
- Tools are discoverable via `list_tools` request
- Tools can be invoked via `call_tool` request

#### **Registration Methods**

1. **Generic type registration** (Native AOT compatible):
   ```csharp
   builder.WithTools<MyTools>()
   builder.WithTools<MyTools>(jsonSerializerOptions)
   ```

2. **Instance-based registration**:
   ```csharp
   builder.WithTools<MyTools>(toolInstance)
   builder.WithTools<MyTools>(toolInstance, jsonSerializerOptions)
   ```

3. **Collection registration**:
   ```csharp
   builder.WithTools(IEnumerable<McpServerTool> tools)
   ```

4. **Type collection registration**:
   ```csharp
   builder.WithTools(IEnumerable<Type> toolTypes, JsonSerializerOptions)
   ```

5. **Assembly-based (reflection)**:
   ```csharp
   builder.WithToolsFromAssembly(assembly, jsonSerializerOptions)
   ```

**JsonSerializerOptions Parameter**:
- Governs tool parameter marshalling/serialization
- Important for proper JSON-RPC parameter handling

---

### **7. Additional Features Discovered**

**Prompts Support** (also available):
- `McpServerPromptAttribute` and `McpServerPromptTypeAttribute` for defining prompts
- Similar registration patterns as tools and resources
- Discoverable via `list_prompts`, invoked via `prompts/get`

**Authorization Support**:
- Add `[Authorize]` attributes to tools, prompts, and resources
- Call `.AddAuthorizationFilters()` on the builder to enable
- Supports standard ASP.NET Core authorization policies

**Session Migration** (distributed scenarios):
- `ISessionMigrationHandler` interface for persisting session state
- Enables session migration across server instances
- Useful for load-balancing and horizontal scaling

**Event Stream Store** (resumability):
- `DistributedCacheEventStreamStore` for SSE resumability
- `.WithDistributedCacheEventStreamStore()` registration method
- Requires `IDistributedCache` implementation (Redis, etc.)

---

### **Key Files Located**

- **ModelContextProtocol.dll**: `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/`
- **ModelContextProtocol.xml**: Same directory (83.5 KB)
- **ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.dll**: `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/`
- **ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.xml**: Same directory (47.9 KB)

---

### **Quick Start Example Structure**

```csharp
// Program.cs
var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);

builder.Services
    .AddMcpServer(options => {
        // Configure server options
    })
    .WithTools<MyTools>()
    .WithResources<MyResources>()
    .AddAuthorizationFilters()
    .WithHttpTransport(opts => {
        opts.Stateless = false;
        opts.IdleTimeout = TimeSpan.FromHours(2);
    });

var app = builder.Build();
app.MapMcp("/mcp");
app.Run();
```

This covers all major APIs for building HTTP-based MCP servers with version 1.2.0 of the NuGet packages.

kind.prompt:
I need to understand the APIs available in the ModelContextProtocol and ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore NuGet packages (version 1.2.0) for building an HTTP-based MCP server in C#.

Please examine these XML doc files and DLLs to answer:

1. What extension methods exist in ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore? Specifically look for `MapMcp` and any HTTP transport setup methods. Check `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.aspnetcore/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.xml`

2. How do you register MCP server services (AddMcpServer)? What options/configuration does it support? Check `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml`

3. How do you define MCP resources? Look for attributes like `McpServerResource`, `McpServerResourceTemplate`, or interfaces for resource handling. Search the XML docs for anything related to "resource".

4. How do you define MCP tools? Look for `McpServerTool` attribute or similar.

5. What is the correct pattern for `AddMcpServer` — does it take a configure callback? What are the options available (ServerInfo, Capabilities, etc.)?

Search both XML files thoroughly. Also run: `strings /home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.2.0/lib/net10.0/ModelContextProtocol.dll | grep -i 'McpServer\|Resource\|MapMcp' | sort -u | head -60` and the same for the AspNetCore DLL to find type names.
